MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-26-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS BEATRIZ AND BRITNEY LOPEZ'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AND TO REOPEN DISCOVERY IS GRANTED ON SPECIFIED CONDITIONS THAT PLAINTIFFS MUST ACCEPT IN WRITING BEFORE THE HEARING OR ORALLY AT THE HEARING AND, IF THEY DOES NOT, THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE CONDITIONS ON THE GRANTING OF THIS MOTION ARE: 1) THE TRIAL IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 18, 2019 AT 9:30AM IN DEPARTMENT 206 WITH ALL DISCOVERY DEADLINES TRIGGERED OFF THE NEW TRIAL DATE; 2) PLAINTIFFS MUST PAY $3,000 TO DEFENDANTS FCA US LLC AND CHRYSLER JEEP DODGE, INC. NO LATER THAN THE DATE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IN THIS CASE, WHICH CAN BE A SETOFF AGAINST ANY DAMAGES AWARDED IN PLAINTIFFS' FAVOR; AND 3) PLAINTIFFS MAY NOT SEEK ATTORNEY'S FEES OR COSTS AGAINST DEFENDANTS FOR ANY TIME OR COSTS INCURRED FOR THIS MOTION AND PREPARATION FOR THE SEPTEMBER 17, 2018 TRIAL DATE, THAT CANNOT BE USED FOR A LATER TRIAL DATE. THE CONDITIONS ARE NECESSARY TO ELIMINATE THE PREJUDICE THAT DEFENDANTS WILL SUFFER BY THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION. $3,000 IS A REASONABLE ESTIMATE FOR THE FEES AND COSTS INCURRED BY DEFENDANTS TO OPPOSE THIS MOTION AND TO PREPARE FOR THE SEPTEMBER 17, 2018 TRIAL DATE, WHICH ARE PROPERLY BORNE BY PLAINTIFFS, NOT DEFENDANTS, DUE TO PLAINTIFFS' LACK OF DILIGENCE IN FILING THIS MOTION. WHILE PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO SHOW THAT HE ACTED DILIGENTLY IN SEEKING TO AMEND HIS COMPLAINT, IF THEY ACCEPT THE CONDITIONS ON THE GRANTING OF THE MOTION, CALIFORNIA'S POLICY OF LIBERALLY ALLOWING AMENDMENTS TO PLEADINGS REQUIRES THAT THE MOTION BE GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR # 8600 =302/HK

Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses
Off Calendar Nov-14-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Amy Rotman, Esq. 858-259-5009 of Wirtz Law APC for Plaintiff.
Georges Haddad, Esq. 415-984-8506 of Clark Hill for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues multipliers. Defendant responds. The Court denies Defendant's request to defer ruling on costs. Costs are awarded in full. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF BEATRIZ BULCAO, BRITNEY LOPEZ'S Motion For Attorneys Fees, Costs And Expenses.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Plaintiffs' motion for attorney's fees, costs and expenses is granted in the amount of $65,163.75 in attorney's fees and $20,830.44 in costs and expenses

(see word minutes for full details)

In addition, Defendant shall pay fees and costs awarded by this order within 45 days of this order. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs BEATRIZ BULCAO and BRITNEY LOPEZ's Motion For Sanctions:

Plaintiffs Beatriz A. Bulcao and Britney B. Lopez's motion for sanctions is denied. Defendants' counsel is admonished to respond timely and professionally to communications from opposing counsel, including inquiries regarding the implementation of negotiated settlement agreements.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs BEATRIZ BULCAO and BRITNEY LOPEZ's Motion To Enforce Settlement

Plaintiffs Beatriz A. Bulcao and Britney B. Lopez's motion to enforce settlement is denied. The letter memorializing the parties' settlement (Morse Decl., Ex. A) was not signed by Plaintiffs. (Code Civ. Proc. 664.6(a) ["If parties to pending litigation stipulate, in a writing signed by the parties outside the presence of the court . . . for settlement of the case, the court, upon motion, may enter judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement." (Emphasis added.)]; Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal.4th 578, 586 ["the term 'parties' as used in section 664.6 . . . means the litigants themselves, and does not include their attorneys of record"]; Harris v. Rudin, Richman & Appel (1999) 74 Cal.App.4th 299, 305 [statute's requirement of a writing "signed by the parties" requires "the signatures of the parties seeking to enforce the agreement under section 664.6 and against whom the agreement is sought to be enforced"].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint And Reopen Discovery
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Jessica R. Underwood of the Wirtz Law APC, Tel. No. 1-858-259-5009 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Michael K. Tcheng of the Clark Hill Law firm, Tel. No. 1-415-984-8564 appearing
for the Defendant.


RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiffs Beatriz and Britney Lopez's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint and to reopen discovery is granted on specified conditions that plaintiffs must accept in writing before the hearing or orally at the hearing and, if they does not, the motion is denied. The conditions on the granting of this motion are: 1) the trial is continued to March 18, 2019 at 9:30am in department 206 with all discovery deadlines triggered off the new trial date; 2) plaintiffs must pay $3,000 to defendants FCA US LLC and Chrysler Jeep Dodge, Inc. no later than the date of entry of judgment in this case, which can be a setoff against any damages awarded in plaintiffs' favor; and 3) Plaintiffs may not seek attorney's fees or costs against defendants for any time or costs incurred for this motion and preparation for the September 17, 2018 trial date, that cannot be used for a later trial date. The conditions are necessary to eliminate the prejudice that defendants will suffer by the granting of the motion. $3,000 is a reasonable estimate for the fees and costs incurred by defendants to oppose this motion and to prepare for the September 17, 2018 trial date, which are properly borne by plaintiffs, not defendants, due to plaintiffs' lack of diligence in filing this motion. While plaintiffs failed to show that he acted diligently in seeking to amend his complaint, if they accept the conditions on the granting of the motion, California's policy of liberally allowing amendments to pleadings requires that the motion be granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =302/hk
